name: Pat Dixon
bio: Pat Dixon is a Texas-based Libertarian politician and business owner who served two terms on the Lago Vista City Council from 2005 to 2010. He previously served as chair of the Libertarian Party of Texas for 10 years and has run for multiple state and federal offices.

stances.position_label: Growth through deregulation and tax cuts; markets lead
stances.summary: Dixon argues that growth should pay for itself through free-market mechanisms, opposes tax-funded corporate incentives, and emphasizes that those who create resource demand should bear the cost.

sources.title: Free Market Sustainability
sources.quote: Free market sustainability means growth pays for itself. It requires those that impact demand for resources to pay for them.

stances.position_label: Lower taxes across the board, including corporations and high earners
stances.summary: Dixon states that no party wants lower taxes more than the Libertarian Party, insists spending cuts must precede tax reductions, and opposes replacing property taxes with higher sales taxes without local control.

sources.title: Property Tax
sources.quote: I don’t have to convince you that there is no political party that wants lower taxes than the Libertarian Party.

stances.position_label: Cut spending significantly to reduce deficits and debt
stances.summary: Dixon pledges to identify specific spending cuts first—citing Texas’ $321 billion budget—and rejects across-the-board cuts without detailing which departments or services would end.

sources.title: Property Tax
sources.quote: If I am elected governor, I will work on identifying spending cuts first, which will lead to lower taxes.

stances.summary: Dixon strongly supports school choice, endorsing statewide voucher programs where education funding follows students to any school. He criticizes standardized testing mandates and 'Robin Hood' school funding redistribution, citing the Texas voucher plan that accepted 90,000 students as a model to expand rather than restrict.

sources.title: School Choice
sources.quote: Ideally, I would like a statewide scholarship. Every child would be given the same amount of money to follow them to any school. This would resolve the Robin Hood (Chapter 41) disputes and remove politically motivated testing mandates.

sources.title: School Choice
sources.quote: I support parents being empowered to choose what is best for their kids. That is hard to do when they have no choice.

stances.summary: Dixon supports welcoming peaceful and productive immigrants, criticizes excessive immigration paperwork, and opposes state-level enforcement that harms people. He acknowledges federal responsibility for border enforcement but refuses to participate in policies that harm Texans' life, liberty, and property.

sources.title: The Border
sources.quote: if you are peaceful and productive, welcome

sources.title: The Border
sources.quote: it is easier to swim the Rio Grande than climb the mountain of paperwork

sources.title: The Border
sources.quote: I would refuse to do so if that meant harming the life, liberty, and property of Texans

stances.summary: As a Libertarian, Dixon's platform emphasizes protecting civil liberties and limiting government power. His statement that Texans should be able to 'do anything you want as long as you are not harming others' and his refusal to support policies harming 'life, liberty, and property' indicate strong civil liberties prioritization over expanded security powers.

sources.title: About Pat
sources.quote: Right sizing government means you can do anything you want as long as you are not harming others

stances.summary: Dixon opposes new AI mandates, arguing private-sector adoption of voluntary standards is preferable to regulation that could slow innovation.

sources.title: AI
sources.quote: Government should avoid mandates and instead allow companies to adopt voluntary standards … heavy regulation risks stifling innovation.

stances.summary: Dixon favors industry self-regulation and voluntary privacy commitments over new federal data-protection statutes.

sources.title: AI
sources.quote: Companies should adopt voluntary standards … rather than imposing new federal rules.

stances.position_label: Paper-ballot integrity, local option for Approval Voting
stances.summary: Dixon supports maintaining voter-ID rules, paper ballots, and hand audits, while also endorsing local adoption of Approval Voting to reduce negative campaigning.

sources.title: Election Integrity
sources.quote: we need to know that voters have the residency and legal status to register … elections in Texas are conducted properly and the results are legitimate.

sources.title: Approval Voting
sources.quote: As governor of Texas, I would support the ability for cities to use better voting systems. My favorite system is Approval Voting.

stances.position_label: Broad libertarian reforms, no specific stock-trading ban
stances.summary: Dixon calls for "non-partisan redistricting" and reducing lobbyist influence but does not detail bans on legislator stock trading or term limits.

sources.title: Non-Partisan Redistricting
sources.quote: gerrymandering … concentrates power in the hands of party bosses rather than voters.

contradictions.description: The stance favors lower taxes but says spending cuts must come before tax reductions, which can conflict with a simple low-taxes-at-all-costs framing.

The label says universal school choice with vouchers, but the summary also includes opposition to centralized testing and school-funding redistribution, mixing school-choice and funding-reform positions.

qualitative.summary: No corruption investigations, indictments, ethics violations, or impeachment proceedings were found in public records. Dixon served 10 years as chair of the Libertarian Party of Texas (2004-2014) and two terms on the Lago Vista City Council (2005-2010) without reported legal issues. However, the 2018 conservative takeover attempt of the Libertarian Party of Texas, which Dixon opposed, suggests internal party conflict though not personal misconduct.

Dixon won two city council elections with 68% and 58% of votes respectively, indicating local electoral success. No approval polling data or constituent service metrics were found. His volunteer work with Central Texas Trail Tamers and St. Mary of the Lake Men's Club demonstrates community engagement, though these are not government accountability measures.

Dixon maintains a personal website with professional resume, government activity archives, and campaign information. His 2026 gubernatorial campaign includes a donation disclosure statement noting reporting to the Texas Ethics Commission. No FOIA compliance records, press access protocols, or complete financial disclosure documents were found in search results.

Dixon held two terms on Lago Vista City Council (2005-2010) and served 10 years as chair of the Libertarian Party of Texas (2004-2014). He also served two terms on the Libertarian National Committee. No record of bills passed as primary sponsor was found; city council archives are referenced but not detailed. His 2020 Texas Senate District 14 candidacy was unsuccessful.

promise_record.promise: Advocate for verifiable paper audit trails in electronic voting systems

promise_source.title: Election Integrity
promise_source.quote: This letter is to express the concern from the City of Lago Vista that the lack of a verifiable paper trail in the voting process is troubling...we urge Travis County to consider offering such voting machines with the option of producing a hard copy ballot

promise_record.action: As Lago Vista City Council member, successfully moved council to send formal letter to Travis County Elections Division demanding paper trail option; later testified at Secretary of State public hearing on Diebold voting systems supporting paper audit trail requirements
promise_record.explanation: Dixon used his elected position to formally advocate for paper trails as promised, sending the council letter and later testifying at the state level.

promise_record.promise: Identify specific spending cuts before promising tax reductions, and respect local control over tax policy

promise_source.title: Property Tax
promise_source.quote: If I am elected governor, I will work on identifying spending cuts first, which will lead to lower taxes. I will also respect local control and the ability for political subdivisions of the state to implement reasonable and responsible tax policy.

promise_record.action: Has not been elected governor; 2026 election has not occurred
promise_record.explanation: Dixon made this promise as a 2026 gubernatorial candidate but has not held the office where he could implement this policy.

promise_record.promise: Oppose conservative takeover of the Libertarian Party of Texas and keep the party libertarian

promise_source.title: Government
promise_source.quote: In 2018 there was an effort by conservatives to overtake the Libertarian Party of Texas. I created http://www.keepthepartylibertarian.org to oppose this effort, and we prevailed.

promise_record.action: Created opposition website keepthepartylibertarian.org and successfully defeated the conservative takeover attempt
promise_record.explanation: Dixon organized opposition to the takeover attempt and explicitly states the effort succeeded in keeping the party libertarian.

promise_record.promise: Address tax policy in budget discussions covering both revenue and spending, not just promise tax cuts without spending cuts

promise_source.title: Property Tax
promise_source.quote: When I was on city council we addressed tax policy in our budget discussion, which is both revenue and spending.

promise_record.action: As Lago Vista City Council member (2005-2010), addressed tax policy within budget discussions covering both revenue and spending
promise_record.explanation: Dixon's past service on city council matches his stated approach of addressing tax policy through comprehensive budget discussions.
